---
title: "The CLI: quartobot"
---

A Python CLI for pre-render and out-of-render work that
`pandoc-manubot-cite` doesn't do (or doesn't do ergonomically). Plus
`usethis`-style scaffolding into existing Quarto projects.

```bash
pip install quartobot
```

`quartobot` depends on `manubot`, so this also puts `pandoc-manubot-cite`
on `PATH`.

::: {.callout-note}
The CLI is in early development. `scan` is implemented; the other
commands described below are scoped but not yet shipped — see the
issue links on each.
:::

## Pre-render commands

### `quartobot scan <path>` { #scan }

Walks `.qmd`/`.md` files under `<path>`, extracts every cite key, classifies
each one (manubot prefix, bare DOI, or hand-curated), groups the results,
and reports duplicates with file:line locations. Pure read. No network.

```
$ quartobot scan .
doi:
  10.1038/s41586-024-12345
  10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128 (3x)
pmid:
  31479462
arxiv:
  2104.10729 (2x)
(hand-curated):
  quarto2024

5 unique key(s), 7 total occurrence(s) across 3 file(s).

Duplicates:
  @doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128:
    intro.qmd:14
    methods.qmd:42
    discussion.qmd:9
```

The scan is heuristic — it strips YAML/TOML frontmatter, fenced code
blocks (```/~~~), and inline code spans before searching, so decoys
like `@fake:notacite` inside backticks won't surface. The
authoritative parse happens at render time inside `pandoc-manubot-cite`.

Exit codes:

- `0` — clean scan, no duplicates.
- `1` — one or more cite keys appear more than once.
- `2` — bad arguments.

That makes it usable as a pre-commit hook.

### `quartobot resolve [<keys>...]` { #resolve }

::: {.callout-warning}
Not yet shipped. Tracked at
[#27](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/issues/27).
:::

Pre-fetch citations via `manubot.cite` and write to `references.json`
(and optionally append BibTeX to `references.bib`). The point isn't to
replace what manubot does at render — it's to do it on a developer's
machine before push, so CI never sees a Crossref hiccup.

```
$ quartobot resolve --from-scan .
Resolving 2 keys...
  doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128  ✓
  pmid:31479462                      ✓
Wrote references.json (2 entries)
Updated _freeze/manubot-cache.json
```

### `quartobot validate <project>` { #validate }

::: {.callout-warning}
Not yet shipped. Tracked at
[#28](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/issues/28).
:::

Pre-flight / CI-lint surface. Every key in the prose resolves cleanly;
`_quarto.yml` declares both bibliographies; `manubot-bibliography-cache`
is set. Exit nonzero on any failure.

```
$ quartobot validate .
✗ @doi:10.1371/nonexistent — unresolved (Crossref 404)
✗ _quarto.yml missing manubot-bibliography-cache
2 issues. Exit 1.
```

## Scaffolding commands

::: {.callout-warning}
Scaffolding commands are not yet shipped. Tracked at
[#25](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/issues/25).
:::

### `quartobot init`

In an existing Quarto project, add the citation extension, merge the
manubot keys into `_quarto.yml`, seed `references.bib`, drop in the
version banner template, write the ten-line `render.yml` wrapper.
Idempotent.

### `quartobot use-render-workflow`

Just the workflow file.

### `quartobot use-banner`

Just the banner template + the `include-before-body` line in
`_quarto.yml`.

### `quartobot use-quarto-yml`

The `_quarto.yml` manubot block (filters, bibliography, `manubot-*`
keys).

### `quartobot detach`

Convert the ten-line reusable-workflow wrapper into the full pipeline
copied out. For users who want to fork the pipeline. After `detach`,
upstream fixes don't flow through.

## Philosophy

Opaque-by-default (reusable workflow): user gets a ten-line `render.yml`
and benefits from upstream fixes. `detach` is the escape hatch when they
outgrow that — they own the full workflow forever after.

The opposite of `r-lib/actions`, which copies 150 lines into every
consumer repo. quartobot's default is friendlier; the escape hatch
matches their model for users who want it.
